content: Hi, I'm Spencer, and I'm a Veterinarian at a rural practice. I've been recovering from a knee injury and I'm looking to learn some new skills to help me stay engaged and active during my recovery.
Sorry to hear about your injury! What kind of skills are you looking to learn, and how's your recovery going so far?

I'm interested in learning more about knitting and crochet. I've always been fascinated by textiles and I think it could be a great way to relax and create something useful. I've been watching tutorials on YouTube and I'm excited to get started.
Knitting and crochet are great hobbies! They can be very therapeutic and rewarding. Have you considered joining a local knitting or crochet group to meet other enthusiasts and learn from them?

I've been looking into some local groups in my area, but I'm not sure if I'm ready to commit to attending meetings. I've been listening to podcasts like 'The Knitting Podcast' and 'Crochet Geek' to learn more about the craft.
Podcasts are a great way to learn and stay motivated! What kind of projects are you interested in making, and do you have a favorite type of yarn or fiber?

I'm interested in making scarves, hats, and blankets. I've been looking at some patterns on Ravelry and I'm excited to try out some new techniques. I've also been listening to audiobooks on my commute to work, and I recently finished 'The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks' by Rebecca Skloot.
Ravelry is a great resource! What's your favorite type of audiobook to listen to, and how do you like to stay organized with your knitting or crochet projects?

I enjoy listening to non-fiction books, especially science and history. I've been using a app called 'Knit Companion' to keep track of my projects and patterns. I've also been watching TV shows like 'The Great British Baking Show' to relax and get inspiration for my knitting.
Non-fiction books can be very engaging! 'The Great British Baking Show' is a great choice for relaxation. Have you considered learning any other skills, such as spinning or dyeing your own yarn?

I've been interested in learning more about spinning and dyeing, but I'm not sure if I have the space or equipment to do it. I've been looking into some local classes or workshops to learn more about it.
Local classes or workshops can be a great way to learn and get hands-on experience. I can provide you with some resources on spinning and dyeing if you're interested.

I'd love to learn more! I've been thinking about attending a workshop at the local fiber arts festival in April. I've also been talking to my friend, Emily, who's an experienced spinner and dyer, and she's offered to teach me some techniques.
That sounds like a great opportunity! Learning from an experienced spinner and dyer can be very valuable. I'm sure you'll have a lot of fun and learn a lot at the festival and from your friend Emily.
